# SC-01: Core Directive Injection

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Inject the signed, immutable Core Directives bundle into every agent context at session start, before any stage work begins. Core directives establish the universal behavioural constraints that govern all agents across all A-SDLC stages. They take absolute precedence over user instructions and cannot be overridden at runtime. The agent must acknowledge receipt before proceeding.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 0 | Cross-Cutting | Mandatory | *Runs at agent session start — before any stage is entered*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated (signed) |
| **Agent does** | Receive and acknowledge the signed core directive payload before any user interaction or stage work begins. Log acknowledgement with timestamp. |
| **Human does** | Security Architect authors, signs, and publishes the core directive payload. Reviews directive violation logs periodically. Triggers re-injection after any directive update. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Core Directives payload | Signed YAML directive bundle from directives/core/core-directives.yaml. |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Core directive acknowledgement | evidence | Confirmation that the agent received and acknowledged the core directive payload, with timestamp and agent identifier. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_session |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

If core directive injection fails or the agent does not acknowledge, no stage work may begin. The session must be terminated and restarted.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(4)(c) | ICT security policies and access controls must govern all automated agents |
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | Protection and prevention of ICT systems must be maintained throughout operations |
| EU AI Act | Art. 14(1) | Human oversight measures must be built into AI system design and operation |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(4) | Protection against unauthorised manipulation through defined access controls |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
